clinical_coherence.notes: 73M Caucasian with HTN, DM2, hyperlipidemia, OSA, CKD3a. Wake-up stroke with right-sided neglect and dysarthria. Vitals appropriate. Clinical presentation coherent. However, the case structure is nearly identical to M01 and M02: wake-up stroke, sinus rhythm on ECG with LAE, paroxysmal AF discovered on prolonged monitoring, mixed mechanism with carotid stenosis.

tool_output_consistency.notes: Generally concordant. MRI shows DWI+/FLAIR+ pattern. ECG shows sinus rhythm with LAE. CTA shows moderate left ICA stenosis. Holter discovers paroxysmal AF with 14.6% burden. Minor discrepancy: homocysteine interpretation mentions 16.8 but value field shows 18.4.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD I63.9. Diagnosis correctly identifies cardioembolic etiology from paroxysmal AF. Critical actions well-defined. The case appropriately notes CKD considerations for contrast and anticoagulant dosing. Differential is reasonable.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Labeled moderate, which is appropriate for the individual case, but nearly identical in structure to M01 and M02 (all three are wake-up strokes with occult paroxysmal AF discovered on Holter). As the third iteration, the difficulty becomes less about diagnostic reasoning and more about pattern recognition. The benchmark would benefit from more diverse moderate cases.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 73M Caucasian with metabolic syndrome is plausible. However, PMH is again nearly identical to M01 and M02: same medication template (amlodipine, losartan, metformin, glipizide, rosuvastatin, aspirin), same comorbidity cluster, same CKD stage. Active smoking adds some differentiation.

educational_value.notes: Solid standard case but highly redundant with M01 and M02. Same core teaching points: DWI-FLAIR mismatch, LAE as AF clue, prolonged monitoring, antiplatelet-to-anticoagulation transition. The higher AF burden (14.6%) and active smoking add minor educational points but do not sufficiently differentiate.

issues_found: Highly redundant with M01 and M02 in structure, PMH, and educational content
Homocysteine value discrepancy between interpretation and data field
Templated PMH/medication pattern across moderate cases
